advice:
The situation has changed since last pick — you already took McBride (or Bowers) in the previous turn based on my prior advice, but let me check your actual roster and current board state carefully. Your roster shows only James Cook so far, and both McBride (VORP +70.85) and Bowers (VORP +67.25) are still on the board. That means the TE advice from last pick still stands — but let me also check the WR landscape more carefully before giving you a final call.

You have 5 picks until your next turn. Three of the four gap teams have already taken a WR. Here's the call:

**Take Trey McBride now.**

Four tight reasons:

1. **TE cliff is brutal and immediate.** McBride (+70.85 VORP) and Bowers (+67.25) are the only two TEs with elite separation from replacement level (123.40 pts). After them, the drop is severe and unrecoverable at a weekly starting position.

2. **Both top TEs will likely be gone in 5 picks.** Their ADPs (20.7 and 20.6) put them squarely in this range. With 5 picks before you're back on the clock, expecting even one to survive is a real gamble.

3. **WR has a much softer cliff.** Amon-Ra St. Brown (+89.84) is the top WR, but Nico Collins (+44.52), A.J. Brown (+43.35), and George Pickens (+41.08) are all still available and represent a shallower VORP drop than the TE fallback after McBride/Bowers.

4. **Zero-RB means your FLEX and bench rounds cover RB later.** You don't need to panic on RB yet — your strategy is built around loading up WR/TE early, and McBride is the single best non-RB value on the board right now.

**Risk of waiting one round:** Very high. Both elite TEs are almost certain to be gone. The next TE tier could cost you 30-40+ VORP points for the entire season at a position you start every week. Don't wait.
